reasoning: Based on the search results, DHI experienced a significant post-earnings drop in late October 2025 and has negative momentum heading into mid-November. Importantly, a $0.45 quarterly dividend was announced, going ex-dividend on November 13, 2025. This creates an immediate structural drop of ~0.3% right before the first resolution date on Nov 16. In the very short term (1 week), the combination of post-earnings negative drift and the known dividend drop drives the probability of being strictly higher than the spot price down to slightly below 50%. Over the 1-month (Dec 9) horizon, the dividend hurdle is the same but the variance increases, meaning the fixed $0.45 drop has less impact on the binary probability, while equity risk premium begins to gently counteract negative momentum. Over the 3-month (Feb 7) horizon, the increased variance (uncertainty) and long-term mean reversion push the binary probability very close to 0.50. To comply with the rule that 'uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5,' I scale the probability progressively closer to 50% for the further dates.
